skill.name: meeting-insights-analyzer
skill.description: Analyzes meeting transcripts and recordings to uncover behavioral patterns, communication insights, and actionable feedback. Identifies when you avoid conflict, use filler words, dominate conversations, or miss opportunities to listen. Perfect for professionals seeking to improve their communication and leadership skills.
skill.summary: Reviews meeting transcripts for communication patterns, facilitation habits, and actionable coaching feedback.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, weak-crypto keywords, and reconnaissance-like words in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are false positives in prose, prompt examples, and output templates, with no executable code, network calls, shell commands, prompt injection, or data exfiltration evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Examples Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the cited lines are inline paths, fenced prompt examples, and Markdown output templates. They instruct the assistant how to analyze meeting transcripts and do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged spans are Markdown examples and instructions, not source code. No shell command syntax or runtime execution path is present in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern in Prose
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm indicators, but the cited lines are ordinary meeting-analysis prose and output labels. No hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API usage appears in those locations.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain skill description and example output wording only. There is no evidence of cryptographic functionality or security-sensitive algorithm selection.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Terms in Communication Guidance
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported system and network reconnaissance, but the cited lines describe identifying communication behaviors and analyzing clarity. No host discovery, network scanning, device enumeration, or similar reconnaissance behavior is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The semantic context is professional communication analysis. The file contains no commands or instructions for collecting system or network information.

content.value_statement: Meeting transcripts can hide repeated habits that affect leadership, clarity, and collaboration. This skill turns transcripts into specific feedback, examples, and improvement actions.
actual_capabilities: Reviews local meeting transcripts for speaking patterns, interruptions, filler words, and listening signals.
Identifies conflict avoidance, hedging language, indirect requests, and missed feedback moments.
Summarizes facilitation style, including question ratios, inclusion of quieter speakers, and decision handling.
Produces timestamped examples when transcripts include timestamps or meeting labels.
Compares multiple meetings or time periods to show communication trends.
limitations: Requires readable transcript files or pasted transcript text from the user.
Cannot verify tone, facial expression, or body language from text-only transcripts.
Accuracy depends on speaker labels, timestamps, and transcript quality.
Does not replace professional coaching, human resources guidance, or legal advice.

use_cases.title: Prepare for a Performance Review
use_cases.description: Collect meeting examples that show communication strengths, growth areas, and specific progress over time.

use_cases.title: Improve Team Facilitation
use_cases.description: Find patterns in speaking balance, question use, decision clarity, and inclusion during recurring team meetings.

use_cases.title: Coach Communication Habits
use_cases.description: Use transcript evidence to discuss filler words, interruptions, directness, and active listening behaviors.

prompt_templates.title: Find One Pattern
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner analysis of a single behavior
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these meeting transcripts for one behavior: filler words. Count examples, show the strongest moments, and suggest three ways to improve.

prompt_templates.title: Review Facilitation Style
prompt_templates.scenario: Standard review of recurring team meetings
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the transcripts in this folder and summarize my facilitation style. Focus on speaking balance, questions, decisions, and inclusion.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Two Time Periods
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare my meetings from Q1 and Q2. Show whether my listening improved, using interruptions, clarifying questions, and examples.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Development Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced coaching output across many meetings
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze all available transcripts and create a communication development plan. Include key patterns, transcript evidence, weekly practice goals, and review metrics.

output_examples.input: Analyze my last five team meetings for conflict avoidance.
output: Finding: You softened direct feedback in four meetings when discussing missed deadlines.
Example: In the roadmap meeting, you suggested revisiting the timeline instead of naming the blocker.
Recommendation: State the issue in the first sentence, then ask one direct question about next steps.

output_examples.input: Compare my facilitation style between two project folders.
output: Trend: Your speaking share dropped from 58 percent to 41 percent across the second folder.
Strength: You asked more clarifying questions and invited quieter participants into the discussion.
Growth area: Action items were still vague in three meetings and need clearer owners.

output_examples.input: Help me prepare communication examples for a performance review.
output: Strength example: You summarized customer concerns clearly before assigning follow-up work.
Growth example: You interrupted twice during a technical disagreement and moved to resolution too quickly.
Next step: Practice pausing after disagreement and ask each participant for one concern.

best_practices: Use consistent file names with dates, meeting names, and transcript source.
Ask for one communication behavior at a time when you need deeper evidence.
Review sensitive transcripts locally and remove personal data that is not needed.
anti_patterns: Do not treat transcript analysis as a complete measure of leadership ability.
Do not analyze private or confidential meetings without appropriate permission.
Do not request broad conclusions when transcripts lack speaker labels or dates.

faq.question: What transcript formats work best?
faq.answer: Plain text, Markdown, VTT, SRT, and exported document text work best when they include speakers and timestamps.

faq.question: Can it analyze meeting recordings directly?
faq.answer: The skill focuses on transcripts. Recordings should be transcribed before analysis.

faq.question: Can it compare progress over time?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide transcripts from different periods and ask for a comparison using the same metrics.

faq.question: Does it send transcripts to external services?
faq.answer: No evidence found in the skill file of network requests or external service calls.

faq.question: Can it create coaching recommendations?
faq.answer: Yes. It can turn observed patterns into practice goals, better phrasing, and follow-up actions.

faq.question: How should sensitive meetings be handled?
faq.answer: Remove unnecessary personal data and analyze only meetings you are authorized to review.
